issues.description: `loadOperatorOverrides()` swallows any `MODULE_NOT_FOUND` whose message mentions `runtime.local` and treats it as if `mini/config/runtime.local.js` were absent. In Node, a broken override file that itself imports a missing dependency produces exactly that message (`Cannot find module './missing-dep.js' ... /runtime.local.js`), so the Mini shell silently falls back to the checked-in placeholder runtime instead of surfacing the operator error. That makes the promised config-driven swap path fail open: a misconfigured local runtime override looks like a successful start, but the app keeps targeting the placeholder backend.

`isLoopbackUrl()` only rejects a few exact host strings (`localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, `0.0.0.0`, `::1`, and `.localhost`). It returns `false` for other loopback spellings such as `https://127.0.0.2` and `https://[::1]` (confirmed with a local Node probe), so the validator/build summary can misclassify loopback targets as safe. That means VAL-PUBLISH-001 / VAL-PUBLISH-006 can be falsely green-lit for checked-in configs that still point at a loopback-only runtime.

codeReview.summary: The feature does introduce a repo-owned placeholder runtime boundary and publish-facing import-shell metadata, but two correctness bugs remain in the runtime-config helper: broken local overrides are silently ignored, and the loopback detector does not actually cover the full set of loopback host spellings that the validator is meant to reject.

issues.description: `loadOperatorOverrides()` swallows any `MODULE_NOT_FOUND` whose message mentions `runtime.local` and treats it as if `mini/config/runtime.local.js` were absent. In Node, a broken override file that itself imports a missing dependency produces exactly that message (`Cannot find module './missing-dep.js' ... /runtime.local.js`), so the Mini shell silently falls back to the checked-in placeholder runtime instead of surfacing the operator error. That makes the promised config-driven swap path fail open: a misconfigured local runtime override looks like a successful start, but the app keeps targeting the placeholder backend.

`isLoopbackUrl()` only rejects a few exact host strings (`localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, `0.0.0.0`, `::1`, and `.localhost`). It returns `false` for other loopback spellings such as `https://127.0.0.2` and `https://[::1]` (confirmed with a local Node probe), so the validator/build summary can misclassify loopback targets as safe. That means VAL-PUBLISH-001 / VAL-PUBLISH-006 can be falsely green-lit for checked-in configs that still point at a loopback-only runtime.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature handoff reports `npm --prefix mini run test`, `build`, and `validate` all passing, but the added runtime-config tests only cover the checked-in `.invalid` default and an in-memory happy-path override object. They never exercise broken `runtime.local.js` loading or alternate loopback spellings, so both bugs remain invisible to the current scrutiny/validator path.

summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, worker handoff/transcript skeleton, validation contract, and commit `a85f6452221d4fd62169727baae88350f50e79dd`. Result: fail — the checked-in Mini import shell generally matches the publish-facing runtime-boundary goal, but the runtime-config helper can silently ignore broken local overrides and can falsely classify some loopback runtime targets as safe.
